opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: I can't walk to the kitchen without getting winded, and I have to prop myself up with extra pillows just to sleep.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: Worsening breathlessness on minimal exertion and at night, needing to sleep propped up, with severe fatigue that has got much worse over two weeks.

symptom_narrative.onset: gradually over 3-4 months, acutely worse over the last two weeks
symptom_narrative.duration: months of increasing breathlessness and fatigue, sharply worse in the past fortnight
symptom_narrative.character: breathlessness on exertion and lying flat, with deep fatigue
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: now struggles with basic daily activities and can't walk to the kitchen without getting winded
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: breathing trouble in the chest; mild ankle swelling by evening
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: worse with exertion and when lying flat; needs three pillows and has an occasional night cough
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: sitting upright/propped up helps his breathing
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: severe fatigue, occasional nocturnal cough, and mild ankle swelling by end of day; denies chest pain and palpitations
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: has been getting more easily short of breath over several months but never this limited
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: declining; struggling with basic activities of daily living

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: lives with an anxious spouse who is worried about his declining stamina and inability to lie flat

psychosocial_profile.hidden_agenda: worried about the rapid decline; spouse pushing for assessment

communication_style.typical_message_style: describes the functional decline clearly; spouse adds concern
